name: Grimm - the Fox And the Cat
body:
It happened that the cat met the fox in a forest, and as she thought to
      herself: âHe is clever and full of experience, and much esteemed in the
      world,â she spoke to him in a friendly way. âGood day, dear Mr Fox, how
      are you? How is all with you? How are you getting on in these hard times?â
      The fox, full of all kinds of arrogance, looked at the cat from head to
      foot, and for a long time did not know whether he would give any answer or
      not. At last he said: âOh, you wretched beard-cleaner, you piebald fool,
      you hungry mouse-hunter, what can you be thinking of? Have you the cheek
      to ask how I am getting on? What have you learnt? How many arts do you
      understand?â âI understand but one,â replied the cat, modestly. âWhat art
      is that?â asked the fox. âWhen the hounds are following me, I can spring
      into a tree and save myself.â âIs that all?â said the fox. âI am master of
      a hundred arts, and have into the bargain a sackful of cunning. You make
      me sorry for you; come with me, I will teach you how people get away from
      the hounds.â Just then came a hunter with four dogs. The cat sprang nimbly
      up a tree, and sat down at the top of it, where the branches and foliage
      quite concealed her. âOpen your sack, Mr Fox, open your sack,â cried the
      cat to him, but the dogs had already seized him, and were holding him
      fast. âAh, Mr Fox,â cried the cat. âYou with your hundred arts are left in
      the lurch! Had you been able to climb like me, you would not have lost
      your life.â
